notes: Exposes the underlying LLM provider name
reason: The wizard's LLM provider is an implementation detail. Naming Claude ties branding to a specific vendor and may confuse users who don't know what Claude is.

notes: Hint says 'debug with Claude' — exposes LLM provider name in user-facing text
reason: Same issue — the hint text on this menu item names Claude explicitly. Should be provider-agnostic since the proxy could change.
suggested_replacement: I'm blocked (keep label; change hint to 'get help debugging')

notes: Exposes LLM provider name (Claude/Anthropic) to users
reason: Names the specific LLM vendor. Users shouldn't need to know which AI provider powers the wizard. Also creates a branding dependency on a third party.

notes: References '.claude/settings.json' — leaks internal agent tooling detail to user
reason: Exposes the internal Claude Agent SDK config path. Most users won't know what .claude/settings.json is. If the underlying agent SDK changes this string becomes stale.
suggested_replacement: A local configuration file is overriding the wizard's network settings.

string: Unify your data with Amplitude Activation
notes: Verify 'Amplitude Activation' is a current product name — may be deprecated or renamed
reason: 'Amplitude Activation' is not a well-known standalone product name on amplitude.com. The data routing product is typically 'Amplitude Data' or 'Amplitude CDP'. Using an incorrect product name misleads users.
suggested_replacement: Remove this tip entirely or replace with a verified product name from amplitude.com/products

string: Collect, clean, and route data to every tool in your stack. One SDK, one source of truth, zero data silos.
notes: Description depends on 'Amplitude Activation' being a real current product
reason: Paired with the unverified product name above — if the product name is wrong the description is also misleading.
suggested_replacement: Remove or rewrite to match whatever the verified product name is

string: Extend your agents with the Skills Marketplace
notes: Verify 'Skills Marketplace' is a shipped/public Amplitude product name
reason: No public 'Skills Marketplace' product exists on amplitude.com as of this audit. Advertising an unshipped or internal-only feature to external users is misleading.
suggested_replacement: Remove this tip entirely until the product is publicly launched

string: Browse and install pre-built skills that teach your agents new tricks — from anomaly detection to automated reporting. Build once, share across your org.
notes: Description depends on Skills Marketplace being a real shipped product
reason: Same as above — describes a product users cannot access.
suggested_replacement: Remove alongside the Skills Marketplace tip

notes: Verify this is a real shipped Amplitude product/feature
reason: No publicly documented 'always-on analytics agent' product exists. This reads like aspirational marketing copy rather than a real feature users can try today.
suggested_replacement: Remove this tip or replace with a link to a verifiable shipped feature

string: Ask questions in Amplitude or in Slack and get instant answers backed by your data. One agent that works wherever your team already lives.
notes: Verify agent-in-Slack is a real shipped feature
reason: Amplitude has Slack notifications but a persistent 'agent' that answers questions in Slack is not a documented GA feature. Claiming it exists misleads users.
suggested_replacement: Remove or rewrite to describe actual Slack integration capabilities (notifications and chart sharing)

string: Amplitude can also help you track your LLM costs
notes: Verify LLM cost tracking is a real shipped Amplitude feature
reason: LLM cost tracking is gated behind a feature flag (FLAG_LLM_ANALYTICS) and may not be GA. Presenting a flag-gated beta feature as a general capability could confuse users if the flag is off.
suggested_replacement: Reword to 'Amplitude can help you track LLM usage' or gate this tip behind the same feature flag

string: From zero to first tracked event in 5 minutes.
notes: Time claim may be inaccurate depending on project complexity
reason: Unverifiable promise — complex projects (Django/Android/Unity) routinely exceed 5 minutes. Sets incorrect expectations.
suggested_replacement: From zero to first tracked event, fast.

string: Go to Settings > Personal Settings > Profile and click "Connect to Slack".
notes: Verify this navigation path is still accurate in current Amplitude UI
reason: Amplitude's settings UI may have reorganized since this was written. If the path is wrong users will be stuck on a screen telling them to click something that doesn't exist.
suggested_replacement: Verify path in production Amplitude UI. If changed, update to match. If unsure, simplify to 'Follow the instructions in the browser to connect Slack.'

string: /feedback — Send product feedback (event: wizard: feedback submitted)
notes: Leaks internal analytics event name to user
reason: The parenthetical '(event: wizard: feedback submitted)' exposes the internal Amplitude event name used for telemetry. Users don't need to see this.

notes: Debug/dev command visible to end users — should be hidden or removed
reason: This is a developer-only test command for exercising wizard-tools:confirm and wizard-tools:choose. Exposing it to end users is confusing. It serves no user purpose.
suggested_replacement: Remove from COMMANDS array entirely (or gate behind --debug flag)

string: Send product feedback (Amplitude event: wizard: Feedback Submitted)
notes: Leaks internal analytics event name in CLI help text
reason: The yargs command description includes the internal tracking event name. This is developer debug info that leaked into user-facing --help output.

notes: Claude status page URL exposed to users via OutageScreen
reason: The status page link shown during outages points to status.claude.com which reinforces the Claude vendor leak. Should either be removed or replaced with a generic status page.
suggested_replacement: Remove or replace with a generic Amplitude status/support URL
